result:
**Connection-point pass**

Personal (non-business): Dan said tourism is changing fast and that Chinese visitors now hire cars and drive — an unprompted observation from his own experience. He mentioned TripAdvisor reviews have fallen and business is slower. He used the "contract salesperson" framing — *you look after me, I'll look after you* — as personal philosophy, not a pitch. He helped Sam tighten the introduction being used on the day (the user's message confirmed this; Sam emailed the improved version later). Geographic: Ridgeline Distillery is on Ridgeline Mountain; Hillside Farm is at the foothills, its foot — genuine neighbours.

Business (set aside): the no-negotiating-on-the-floor advice.

**Ask audit**

The email wants nothing back. The give is a genuine neighbour invitation. No referral swap, no "point guests our way." That passes the test; there is a human note to write here.

---

[[B1]]

to: info@example.com
subject: Good to meet you at the tower

Dan,

Good to find another hinterland operation in that room — I hadn't expected it.

You sharpened the way I was describing our place to the hoteliers, and I used it for the rest of the session. Thank you for that.

We're at the foothills, just down from Ridgeline. If you and the family ever want to come out for a ride and a look around, you'd be very welcome.

Sam
